title: Meeting Rooms
description: Given an array of meeting time intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], determine if a person could attend all meetings.
hints: Sort the intervals by start time.
Check if any interval's start is less than the previous interval's end.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals, and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals that cover all the intervals in the input.
hints: Sort the intervals by their start value.
Use a result list to keep track of merged intervals.
For each interval, if it overlaps with the last merged interval, merge them.

title: Insert Interval
description: You are given an array of non-overlapping intervals intervals sorted by their start values, and a new interval. Insert the new interval into intervals such that intervals is still sorted and non-overlapping. Return the resulting array of intervals.
hints: Add all intervals that end before the new interval starts.
Merge all intervals that overlap with the new interval.
Add all intervals that start after the new interval ends.
Handle edge cases where the new interval merges multiple existing intervals.

solutions.python:
def insert(intervals, newInterval):
    result = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(intervals) and intervals[i][1] < newInterval[0]:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1
    while i < len(intervals) and intervals[i][0] <= newInterval[1]:
        newInterval[0] = min(newInterval[0], intervals[i][0])
        newInterval[1] = max(newInterval[1], intervals[i][1])
        i += 1
    result.append(newInterval)
    while i < len(intervals):
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1
    return result

title: Meeting Rooms II
description: Given an array of meeting time intervals intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], return the minimum number of conference rooms required.
hints: Use a min-heap to track the end times of ongoing meetings.
For each meeting, if the earliest ending meeting has finished, reuse that room.
The heap size at any point represents the number of concurrent meetings.

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], return the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.
hints: Sort intervals by their end time.
Greedy: always keep the interval with the earliest end time.
Count how many intervals overlap and need to be removed.

title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
description: There are some spherical balloons taped onto a flat wall that represents the XY-plane. The balloons are represented as a 2D integer array points where points[i] = [xstart, xend] denotes a horizontal balloon whose horizontal diameter stretches between xstart and xend. You do not know the exact y-coordinates of the balloons. Arrows can be shot up directly vertically in the positive y-direction from different points along the x-axis. A balloon with xstart and xend is burst by an arrow shot at x if xstart <= x <= xend. There is no limit to the number of arrows that can be shot. A shot arrow keeps traveling up infinitely, bursting any balloons in its path. Given the array points, return the minimum number of arrows that must be shot to burst all balloons.
hints: Sort the balloons by their end position.
Use a greedy approach: always shoot the arrow at the end of the first balloon.
If a balloon starts after the current arrow position, shoot a new arrow.

title: Interval List Intersections
description: Given two lists of closed intervals, firstList and secondList, each list of intervals is pairwise disjoint and in sorted order. Return the intersection of these two interval lists.
hints: Use two pointers to iterate through both lists simultaneously.
For each pair of intervals, find the overlap by taking max of starts and min of ends.
Move the pointer of the interval that ends first.

title: Remove Covered Intervals
description: Given a list of intervals intervals, all the intervals are unique, return the number of intervals that are removed. An interval [a, b] is covered by another interval [c, d] if c <= a and b <= d.
hints: Sort intervals by start ascending, and by end descending when starts are equal.
Track the maximum end seen so far.
If the current interval's end is less than or equal to the max end, it is covered.

title: The Skyline Problem
description: A city's skyline is the outer contour of the silhouette formed by all the buildings in that city when viewed from a distance. Given the locations and heights of all the buildings, return the skyline formed by these buildings collectively. The input is given as a list of buildings where buildings[i] = [lefti, righti, heighti]. The skyline is represented as a list of key points sorted by their x-coordinate in the form [[x1, y1], [x2, y2], ...]. Each key point is the left endpoint of some horizontal segment in the skyline except the last point, which always has a y-coordinate of 0.
hints: Use the sweep line algorithm with a multiset (or max-heap) to track active building heights.
At each x-coordinate, add new buildings and remove ended buildings.
A key point is added when the maximum height changes.
Use a multiset to handle duplicate heights correctly.

title: Employee Free Time
description: We are given a list schedule of employees, which represents the working time for each employee. Each employee has a list of non-overlapping intervals representing their busy time. Return a list of finite intervals representing the free time common to all employees.
hints: Merge all busy intervals from all employees into a single sorted list.
After merging, gaps between consecutive intervals are the free time.
The result should not include intervals before the first busy or after the last busy.
